package transport

import (
	"database/sql"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for nullable_sql_types_leak_into_api_dto.
// Anti-pattern: API DTO exposes sql.Null* types, leaking persistence
// implementation details into the public API contract.

type UserResponse struct {
	ID        uint           `json:"id"`
	Username  string         `json:"username"`
	Email     sql.NullString `json:"email"`
	Bio       sql.NullString `json:"bio"`
	LastLogin sql.NullTime   `json:"last_login"`
	BirthDate sql.NullTime   `json:"birth_date"`
	Score     sql.NullInt64  `json:"score"`
	Verified  sql.NullBool   `json:"verified"`
}

func GetUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var user UserResponse
	if err := db.Where("id = ?", c.Param("id")).First(&user).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "user not found"})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: response DTO contains sql.Null* types.
	// JSON serialization produces awkward output like:
	//   "email": {"String": "user@example.com", "Valid": true}
	//   "last_login": {"Time": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", "Valid": false}
	// instead of clean API values like:
	//   "email": "user@example.com"
	//   "last_login": null
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, user)
}
